article_title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 as It Tests Safer Cyber Guardrails Ahead of Mythos
summary: Submission needs source provenance cleanup before publication

findings.message: Body cites a source URL that is missing from article.sources
findings.details: The article body cites https://red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview/ for the Mythos Preview / Project Glasswing claim, but that URL is not included in article.sources. Every source used in the body needs to be listed in the submission's sources array so the provenance snapshot is complete.

content_preview.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7 as It Tests Safer Cyber Guardrails Ahead of Mythos
content_preview.summary: Opus 4.7 lands across Claude, Bedrock, Vertex AI, and Foundry with unchanged pricing, while Anthropic uses it to trial new cyber safeguards before any broader Mythos rollout.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Anthropic released Claude Opus 4.7 on April 16, describing it as its latest generally available model and saying it improves advanced software engineering, vision, instruction-following, and professional document work over Opus 4.6, according to [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-7?fp=1). The model is available to Claude Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise users, and through the Claude Platform, Amazon Bedrock, Google Cloud Vertex AI, and Microsoft Foundry, with pri...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
Add the cited red.anthropic.com Mythos source to article.sources or remove that citation from the body and rely only on listed sources

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is strong on the product-versus-safeguards framing and stays appropriately cautious about Mythos availability. The structure is clear and the analysis is proportionate.
editor_notes.source_verification: I read all five listed sources directly and also checked the additional red.anthropic.com Mythos Preview page that the body cites. Anthropic's Opus 4.7 announcement supports the release date, availability, unchanged pricing, xhigh effort level, task budgets, /ultrareview, and the use of Opus 4.7 to test cyber safeguards ahead of broader Mythos-class rollout. The product page and Economic Times support the channel availability and unchanged pricing. The Verge supports the positioning of Opus 4.7 relative to Mythos. TechCrunch supports the earlier Mythos / Project Glasswing framing. The extra red.anthropic.com page also supports the Mythos Preview cybersecurity-capability claim, but it is not declared in article.sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The factual claims are broadly sound, but the submission has a provenance defect because one cited source is missing from the declared sources list.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. The article does not overstate Anthropic's safety claims.
editor_notes.originality: Originality check passed. This is a distinct follow-on from prior Mythos leak coverage rather than a duplicate.
concerns: The body cites an undeclared source URL, which breaks the expected provenance chain for review and publication.
recommendations: Add https://red.anthropic.com/2026/mythos-preview/ to article.sources and resubmit, or remove that citation from the body and rely only on already listed sources.
If the source is added, keep the rest of the article largely unchanged.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Request changes. The story is otherwise ready, but the source list must fully cover the sources cited in the body.
